title: Fuel price hike: FM says 'India can't afford fear mongering'; govt may lose Rs 1 lakh cr revenue
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that reducing fuel excise duties would cost Rs 1 lakh crore, emphasizing India's need for confidence over fearmongering amidst rising petrol and diesel prices. She highlighted the resilience of India's domestic economy despite global pressures on fuel, fertilizer, and forex, urging focus on these "three Fs".

title: Coimbatore girl rape-murder: Cops caught grinning during media briefing
content: A senior Tamil Nadu police officer is facing backlash after a video showed her laughing before a press briefing on a brutal girl's rape-murder case. The clip sparked outrage, with many questioning the perceived insensitivity. This controversy follows similar criticism of a state minister for her demeanor during media interactions on the same tragic incident.

title: Inside story of Gill's T20 redemption: 1000s of balls with middler bat and unlocking the 'V'
content: Dropped from India’s 2026 T20 World Cup squad, Shubman Gill returned to his home in Mohali determined to reinvent his T20 game. Through relentless training, minute adjustments and hours with a middler bat, Gill developed greater range, control and power, laying the foundation for his most complete and commanding IPL season yet.

title: Man kills brother, grandmother and uncle over land feud in Haryana
content: A shocking land dispute turned deadly in Ambala as a 22-year-old allegedly shot his elder brother, grandmother, and uncle dead, critically injuring his aunt.  The accused, Abhishek, reportedly opened fire during a heated argument over sand sale proceeds.  Police are actively searching for him and investigating the source of the weapon.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai on AI fears: ‘We have to be very mindful’
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai expressed optimism for the next generation's ability to navigate AI's impact, despite growing student concerns about job displacement. He believes graduates will both drive and adapt to technological advancements. Pichai acknowledged public anxiety, stating the industry must better demonstrate AI's benefits and address infrastructure needs.

title: Why is Delhi Gymkhana Club being asked to vacate? Congress sees Rahul Gandhi angle
content: Congress leader Rashid Alvi has slammed the Centre's order for the Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its premises, alleging the move is politically motivated due to Rahul Gandhi's membership. The government cites urgent public purpose and national interest for reclaiming the land, a decision the club wishes to discuss to avoid disruption.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Indian Army makes its artillery arm more lethal by giving its guns automatic aiming capability
content: The Indian Army is enhancing its 105mm field guns with automatic aiming technology, a significant modernization step. This computer-driven system drastically reduces manual effort, improving accuracy, response times, and survivability for artillery crews. This upgrade aligns with the broader Field Artillery Rationalisation Plan, aiming for a more potent and efficient artillery force.

title: Gukesh breaks silence on criticism: 'They have the right to say'
content: D Gukesh admitted criticism over his recent poor form is fair, saying, “They have the right to say what they feel and I have the right to do my best.” Since winning the world title against Ding Liren in 2024, Gukesh has struggled for consistency, drawing criticism from legends like Magnus Carlsen and Anatoly Karpov.

title: NEET paper leak row: SC says NTA 'hasn’t learned its lesson', issues notice to Centre
content: The Supreme Court has raised serious concerns regarding the National Testing Agency's continual mishaps with paper leaks, indicating a troubling pattern of negligence. While assessing various appeals for changes in the examination process, the highest court has instructed the NTA and the central government to deliver a detailed response.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Twisha's mom-in-law and retired judge Giribala Singh to argue her own case in HC
content: A vacation bench of the MP High Court will hear a petition on Monday seeking cancellation of anticipatory bail for retired district judge Giribala Singh. She is accused in the dowry death case of her daughter-in-law, Twisha Singh. Twisha's family approached the High Court after the retired judge was granted bail by a Bhopal sessions court.

title: Timeline of petrol, diesel price hike: How rates rose by Rs 7.5 litre in just 11 days
content: The successive hikes come after an extended period during which retail fuel prices remained unchanged, despite higher global crude prices, shrinking refining margins, and weakness in the rupee significantly increasing import costs.

title: Bizarre! Rapper Travis Scott dragged into Virat Kohli-Travis Head IPL storm
content: A bizarre mix-up has seen fans troll American rapper Travis Scott, mistaking him for cricketer Travis Head due to their shared first name. This online chaos follows an on-field spat between Head and Virat Kohli during an IPL match. The abuse has extended to Head's wife and family, highlighting the dark side of fan culture and the need for empathy.

title: UPSC Prelims 2026 analysis: GS paper tough, unconventional; experts predict lower cutoff
content: UPSC CSE Prelims 2026, conducted on May 24, has been described by experts as one of the most difficult and unconventional papers in recent years. With lengthy comprehension-based questions, analytical framing, and unpredictable current affairs themes, the exam tested conceptual clarity and decision-making skills over rote learning. While several experts predicted a lower cutoff this year, others viewed the paper as a necessary shift towards evaluating aptitude, adaptability, and intellectual curiosity expected from future civil servants.

title: Women with muscles are challenging traditional ideas of femininity: Fitness expert
content: Fitness expert Dr. Ambika Dutt observes a significant shift as women increasingly embrace strength training, challenging traditional notions of femininity.  This movement prioritizes health, energy, and confidence over outdated beauty standards.  Despite facing criticism for appearing 'too muscular,' women, especially mothers, are reclaiming their bodies and well-being, demonstrating that strength and femininity can powerfully coexist.

title: Norway Chess: Gukesh’s test, Humpy's return — Why should you be excited?
content: As Norway Chess moves to Oslo for its 2026 edition, all eyes are on the Indian contingent. From World Champion D Gukesh seeking urgent form before his title defence to Koneru Humpy making a highly anticipated return to the board, we break down the biggest storylines dominating the 12-day double round-robin tournament.

title: Honda City facelift vs Hyundai Verna facelift: Price, features compared
content: Design-wise, the new Honda City adopts a sharper design language, with a flatter, more upright front end compared to the current model. The biggest highlight of the front end is the connected light strip and Honda’s new logo. The updated styling draws inspiration from Honda’s newer global products, featuring cleaner surfaces and a more modern look.

title: 'Their issue is different': Rubio on whether India is uneasy about Pak's role in US-Iran conflict
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that India did not express concerns regarding Pakistan's mediation role in the US-Iran conflict. While India remains focused on terrorist groups operating from Pakistani soil, this specific diplomatic engagement by Islamabad did not arise as a point of discussion.

title: ‘Mummy, did you see the race?’ India’s fastest man’s 1st call home after 10.09s sprint
content: Gurindervir Singh has made headlines by breaking the national 100m record with an electrifying time of 10.09 seconds. This achievement not only secures his place in the upcoming Commonwealth and Asian Games but also turns him into a local hero.

title: LSG autopsy: How Pant’s woes, Indian core's frailties became a disaster in the making
content: Lucknow Super Giants finished bottom of the IPL 2026 table after another disappointing season marked by inconsistency, middle-order collapses and poor bowling. Injuries and the absence of Wanindu Hasaranga hurt team balance badly. Rishabh Pant and Nicholas Pooran underperformed, while Josh Inglis and Mitchell Marsh were among the few positives for LSG.

title: IPL 2026: Dhauladhar views, playoffs mood - Gujarat Titans’ Dharamsala day out
content: On Sunday, the Gujarat Titans trained with full intensity ahead of their Qualifier 1 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) at the Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association (HPCA) Stadium in Dharamsala. Captain Shubman Gill and pacer Kagiso Rabada were notable absentees from the three-hour training session.

title: 'Home to many UN agencies': WHO office in Ukraine damaged after multiple strikes by Russia
content: The World Health Organization's Kyiv office sustained damage from debris during recent Russian strikes, prompting the WHO Director-General to renew calls for a ceasefire. The building, housing multiple UN agencies, saw shattered windows but no injuries. These attacks, which killed at least four civilians and injured others, follow Moscow's vow of retaliation for Ukrainian strikes.

title: ‘A tough season’: Emotional Pant video goes viral amid LSG captaincy uncertainty
content: Rishabh Pant's emotional thank you to fans after Lucknow Super Giants' disastrous IPL 2026 campaign has gone viral amid leadership change speculation. The team's bottom-place finish and Pant's struggles with captaincy and batting have intensified scrutiny, with the franchise reportedly considering major decisions.

title: Shaun Romy speaks about Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' alongside 'Karakkam' release
content: Actress Shaun Romy expresses excitement for Malayalam cinema's festive season, highlighting the contrasting releases of 'Drishyam 3' and her upcoming musical horror-comedy 'Karakkam'. She praises Mohanlal's inspiring presence and shares details about 'Karakkam', a light-hearted supernatural comedy with a strong musical element. Meanwhile, 'Drishyam 3' continues its impressive worldwide box office run.

title: 2027 WC: Australia backs ageing Big Three as Team India faces Kohli-Rohit dilemma
content: Cricket Australia has publicly backed its veteran pace trio for the 2027 ODI World Cup, prioritising experience despite fitness concerns. In stark contrast, Team India faces uncertainty regarding Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli, and Ravindra Jadeja's futures, hinting at a significant transition phase for the national team as the next World Cup looms.

title: Can your ex-husband stop paying alimony if you remarry?
content: Indian law generally dictates that a wife's remarriage terminates her ex-husband's maintenance obligation. This principle applies across various laws, including the new Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita and the Hindu Marriage Act. However, a formal court order is necessary to cease payments, and child maintenance remains unaffected.

title: AP EAMCET 2026 answer key released; raise objections by May 27
content: The Andhra Pradesh State Council of Higher Education (APSCHE) has released the provisional AP EAMCET 2026 answer key and response sheets on its official website. Candidates can raise objections to the provisional key until May 27, 2026, with a fee of Rs 300 per question. The final answer key and results are expected by June 1, 2026.

title: WATCH: Aaradhya's sweet reaction to mom Aishwarya's moment with a fan
content: Aaradhya Bachchan's sweet reaction to seeing her mother, Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, interact with fans at Cannes has gone viral. The mother-daughter duo also made a stylish red carpet appearance, with Aaradhya in a satin ensemble and Aishwarya stunning in a pink gown. Aishwarya's fashion choices throughout the festival have been a major talking point.

title: 'They'd never unblock me': Warner, blocked by SRH, reopens old wounds
content: David Warner claimed Sunrisers Hyderabad still has him blocked on Instagram, replying to a fan, “They would never unblock me.” The exchange revived memories of Warner’s bitter exit from SRH in 2021 after being removed as captain and dropped from the team. Despite the fallout, Warner remains one of SRH’s greatest players, leading them to the 2016 IPL title.

title: Russia’s Oreshnik: What makes multi-warhead ballistic missile used against Ukraine so hard to stop
content: Russia unleashed a fierce assault on Kyiv, deploying the nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic missile alongside numerous drones and other advanced weaponry. This escalation followed Moscow's accusations of Ukrainian strikes on a student dormitory, which Kyiv denied.

title: Fuel prices hiked again: Petrol, diesel rates raised by over Rs 2; 4th increase in 2 weeks
content: Fuel prices have seen another significant jump, with petrol and diesel costs rising by Rs 2.61 and Rs 2.71 per litre respectively. This marks the fourth increase in under two weeks, pushing cumulative hikes close to Rs 7.5 per litre.  Consumers face mounting pressure as import costs climb due to global crude oil prices and a weaker rupee.

title: DU girl drugged, gang-raped multiple times, abandoned at Lucknow station with general bogie ticket
content: A 19-year-old student alleges she was drugged and gang-raped over four days in Lucknow by three youths, two of whom she knew from her village. Persuaded to break her journey from Jaunpur to Delhi, she was taken to a rented room where the assaults allegedly occurred. Police have formed multiple teams to investigate the case.

title: ‘Back and forth’ delays Iran deal, White House says agreement still not sealed: Report
content: US officials express caution regarding a potential Iran deal, suggesting it could still collapse despite progress. Negotiations are ongoing, with key details like uranium disposal and frozen assets remaining points of contention.  While Iran aims to reassure the world about its nuclear program, both sides emphasize safeguarding national interests, with a breakthrough potentially days away.

title: Quote of the day by Liam Neeson
content: Liam Neeson, a symbol of resilience, transformed from theatre stages to global stardom, embodying a profound philosophy of perseverance.  His journey, marked by critical acclaim and personal tragedy, highlights the power of picking oneself up after rejection.  Neeson's enduring mindset, forged through years of struggle, offers a timeless lesson on navigating life's inevitable setbacks and embracing unexpected opportunities.

title: IMD issues heatwave alert for Vidarbha; Western Maharashtra and Marathwada to witness light rains
content: Indian Metrological Department (IMD) has sent heat wave alert for Vidarbha region, including Amravati, Wardha, and Chandrapur districts for two days

title: ‘Needless fuss’: Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan defends posting former State poll chief Rathan Kelkar as his Secretary
content: Satheesan says neither CPI(M), which was in power earlier, nor BJP had found fault with Kelkar’s service as Chief Electoral Officer (Kerala)

title: Scorching days, restless nights: Heatwave tightens grip across parts of India
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) warned that temperatures across Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh are likely to rise further from Sunday (May 24) onwards

title: Punjab revises office, school timings amid heatwave; employees welcome move
content: Employees praised the initiative, stating the move would help reduce excessive use of air conditioners and lower electricity demand during peak daytime temperatures

title: Citizens, activists question flyover-led development near Hyderabad’s KBR Park
content: Cities in other nations which tackled congestion without relying heavily on additional flyovers was cited at an event held in Hyderabad

title: Stray dogs case: SC refuses to entertain plea over Punjab CM's statement
content: Just because a Chief Minister makes a statement, does that mean we have to change our order, a bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta asked the counsel

title: Fuel price hike increases cultivation costs, burdens farmers: Telangana Transport Minister
content: BJP’s ‘Rythu Gosa – BJP Bharosa’ yatra a political drama amid pressing issues faced by farmers and common man, says Ponnam Prabhakar

title: CM directs heightened vigilance during Eid-ul-Azha; reviews security in sensitive U.P. districts
content: He directed that namaz be offered only at traditional venues, and said under no circumstances should permission be granted to offer prayers by blocking roads

title: Gold smuggling under focus as agencies detect rise in attempts after import duty hike
content: A Customs officer said teams rely heavily on profiling, behavioural analysis and intelligence inputs to identify suspects

title: Bakrid holiday in Kerala on May 27 and 28
content: Government had declared holiday for Bakrid on May 27. However, as Bakrid is being celebrated in Kerala on May 28, decision is taken to extend the holiday

title: Pawan Kalyan orders action plan to stop sewage, effluents entering Godavari near Pushkar Ghat
content: Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan inspects the river by punt, learns only 15% of the city’s sewage is treated, and sets ‘zero pollution’ before the 2027 Pushkarams as the goal

title: Supreme Court grants bail to Chhattisgarh's former excise commissioner in two liquor policy cases
content: A Bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipup M. Pancholi granted the bail

title: Twisha Sharma death: SC pained by handling of case, asks media to exercise restraint; CBI team dispatched to Bhopal
content: Better to have a divorced daughter than face such an unfortunate incident, says Solicitor General during SC hearing

title: Kerala ramps up pre-monsoon health preparedness to combat infectious diseases
content: Health Minister K. Muraleedharan says government MCHs have been directed to make necessary arrangements so that ‘systemic failures’ do not result in disruption of surgeries in hospitals

title: Odisha bars labour work during peak heat hours
content: Field-level officials have also been instructed to remain vigilant and spread awareness among contractors and workers to prevent any untoward incidents caused by extreme heat conditions

title: Apiculture farmers in Karnataka hit by low budgetary allocation
content: Low budgetary allocation has forced the department to provide only two to three beekeeping boxes to farmers instead of providing minimum 10 boxes to each under the State Apiculture Development Scheme.

title: ₹10 overcharge: At TASMAC outlets, low-paid workers bear cost of administrative failure
content: Before the elections, TVK chief C. Joseph Vijay, during a rally, took a jibe at the charging ₹10 above the maximum retail price (MRP).extra ₹10 charged at TASMAC outlets. Workers at the shops do not dispute the practice, but say it merely offsets irregularities in the system

title: CUET-UG exams scheduled for May 28 postponed due to Bakrid; revised dates soon
content: The National Testing Agency said the decision was taken in view of the Department of Personnel and Training's office memorandum regarding the revised date of the Bakrid holiday

title: Couple killed in lightning strike near Gobichettipalayam
content: The police said Suresh, 42, and Manjula, 38, were at their tin-roofed shop situated beneath a tamarind tree when heavy rain accompanied by thunder and lightning lashed the area around 6.10 p.m. on Sunday

title: Patients shifted as rainwater inundates Rasipuram Government Hospital
content: Rainwater entered the women’s ward, following which hospital authorities shifted patients from the inpatient and maternity wards to alternative rooms using wheelchairs as a precautionary measure

title: IMD forecasts rain for south Bengal from Wednesday
content: The India Meteorological Department said there will be a gradual fall in maximum temperature across West Bengal from May 28 by two to three notches

title: Bengal CM holds second 'janata darbar', hears public grievances
content: Hundreds of people, many of whom are job seekers, were seen queuing up at the party office since morning to interact with the CM

title: Couple killed as car submerges in flooded railway underpass near Omalur
content: Heavy rain lashed Omalur and surrounding areas through Sunday (May 24) night, resulting in severe waterlogging at the railway underpass near Enathi village at Semmandapatti

title: ‘Mehangai manav’ strikes again: Rahul slams PM on fuel hike
content: Petrol and diesel prices were raised by ₹2.61-2.71 per litre on May 25, marking the fourth increase in less than two weeks as state-owned fuel retailers continued to pass on rising international oil prices to consumers

title: Teen dies after zipline cable snaps at Agra amusement facility
content: The boy was on the zipline ride when the cable allegedly snapped midway causing him to fall nearly 45 feet, the police said

title: ‘Don’t take it so sentimentally’: CJI tells lawyer who complained about ‘activities’ of Cockroach Janta Party, fake law degrees
content: Chief Justice Kant said there was “no grave urgency” to entertain a writ petition filed by an apex court lawyer seeking a probe into the “activities” of a “digital-political formation”, Cockroach Janta Party, and the commercial exploitation, trademark appropriation and monetised circulation of oral remarks made in court proceedings.

title: Fire at Undavalli hill near CM’s camp office brought under control
content: CM Naidu closely monitored the situation and spoke over the phone with Guntur District Collector C.M. Sai Kanth Varma and senior fire services officials regarding the incident

title: Runway edge lights treated as runway in Air India Express take off accident in Oman
content: Oman’s air accident investigation body has reported that the occurrence was rated an accident and is being investigated.

title: Deeply distressing: J&K CM on Budgam minor rape case
content: I condemn this brutal attack without reservation or condition and send my sympathies to her family, says Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah

title: MLA Sridhar Reddy’s daughter Sai Vaishnavi enters politics, vows TDP win in Nellore
content: At her first press conference, Kotamreddy Sai Vaishnavi steps forward for the TDP in Nellore Rural, where her ailing father is the MLA and her uncle now runs party affairs

title: Fourth petrol, diesel price hike in 10 days hits Kerala hard
content: Since the first hike on May 15, petrol prices have increased by ₹8.01 and diesel by ₹7.93 across Kerala, with slight variations in regions

title: Who is Abhijeet Dipke? Inside the viral Cockroach Janta Party movement
content: What began as a satirical response to Chief Justice of India Surya Kant’s oral observation has grown into an online movement reflecting youth anger, political satire, and distrust in institutions

title: Modi govt has sprinkled petrol to burn common people's savings: Kharge
content: With the latest revision, cumulative increases in petrol and diesel prices have nearly touched ₹7.5 per litre since fuel price revisions resumed on May 15 after a prolonged freeze

title: Students suggested to focus on skill oriented courses
content: K.S.B.K. Raju gives away certificates to 20 students who finished short-term computer courses, and says the institution will soon start internships for polytechnic and engineering students

title: TVK regime looks to maximise revenue without increasing taxes
content: While the ruling party, in its manifesto, has promised several welfare measures and freebies to people, it is alsogetting conscious of the fiscal position in which the government finds itself in

title: NIA raids Darul Uloom, other locations in J&K's Srinagar, Shopian
content: Officers say another NIA team carried out searches at the residence of the former chief of the banned Jamaat-e-Islami

title: ‘Don’t be complacent’: BJP national president Nitin Nabin tells Karnataka unit while citing T.N. example of Opposition not benefitting from DMK’s failure
content: BJP national president urges Karnataka State unit to instil confidence among people that it is a better political alternative

title: Latur farmer claims daughter ended life over NEET-UG paper leak; police launch probe
content: Farmer blames NEET-UG paper leak for daughter’s death; police investigate claims of mental stress as cause

title: UCC Bill introduced in Assam Assembly
content: If the Bill is passed, Assam will become the third State in the country to pass a UCC Bill, following Uttarakhand, and Gujarat

title: Pune school principal shared NEET-related questions for money: CBI tells court
content: Following Manisha Sanjay Havaldar’s arrest, she was produced before a court in Pune and is currently in transit remand; she is likely to be produced in a Delhi court on May 25

title: BJP MLA Mahesh Tenginakai insulted migrant workers in Hubballi assuming they are infiltrators, says Congress leader Rajat Ullagaddimath
content: Congress leader criticises police officers, who were present, accusing them of failing to follow the rule of law

title: Sharmila slams govt. for filing cases against noted academic
content: In a statement, Ms. Sharmila said it was unbecoming of the NDA government in the State to target an academic and intellectual in such a manner

title: Police protection provided at home of Cockroach Janta Party founder
content: The protection is to ensure there is no crowding around his residence as the CJP is trending online, police official says

title: I wasn’t allowed to see her body, mother of murdered girl alleges
content: The mother of the 10-year-old girl, who was sexually assaulted and killed near Coimbatore, said her husband took the child’s body to his native place in Salem and cremated it without her consent or allowing her to see the mortal remains

title: CBSE again extends deadline for scanned answer book requests till May 25
content: The CBSE had earlier extended the deadline from May 22 to May 23, then till May 24, and has now further extended it till May 25 midnight amid complaints from students over difficulties in accessing scanned copies of answer books through the portal

title: No Hearse Available, Telangana Family Carries Body On Shoulders For 4 Km
content: Kandi Venkanna (45) - a resident of Telangana's Bhadrachalam - suffered heatstroke last week after being unable to bear the intense heat.

title: 3 Dead, 17 Missing As Unfinished Hotel Collapses in Philippines
content: A day after the unfinished building collapse with a loud crashing sound after a fierce thunderstorm, Angeles City Mayor Carmelo Lazatin said rescue efforts would still not be shifted to a body retrieval operation.

title: Class 9 Tripura Boy Lures 6-Year-Old Neighbour With Flowers At Home, Rapes Her
content: The incident took place on Saturday when the girl was at home with her elder sister, and their parents were away to cut grass.

title: Singha Beer Heir Sexual Abuse Allegations Ignite Rare Public Reckoning In Thailand
content: One of Thailand's biggest beer brands, Singha is recognised internationally for its golden lion logo and partnerships with sports giants Chelsea FC and the Haas Formula One team.

title: Pilgrims Kick Off Hajj As War's Trajectory Hangs In Balance
content: The start of the hajj came as US President Donald Trump continued to send mixed signals over a possible agreement to extend an uneasy ceasefire with Iran and a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 'Divorced Daughter Better Than Dead': Top Law Officer In Twisha Sharma Case
content: Twisha Sharma's husband, Samarth Singh, who went missing right after his wife's death, surrendered before the cops after 10 days.

title: "True Treasure Of World": Marco Rubio Praises Taj Mahal During Visit With Wife
content: The Taj Mahal, one of the seven wonders of the world, continues to attract high-profile global leaders and dignitaries visiting India.

title: Teen Killed In Madurai Temple Parking Lot, BJP Attacks Chief Minister Vijay
content: The incident comes barely days after a 10-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, sexually assaulted and brutally murdered near Sulur in Coimbatore district.

title: Indian Sailors Risk Work At Sea As Iran War Grinds On: 'It's Well Paid Job'
content: India is one of the largest contributors of sailors on merchant shipping worldwide, with more than 320,000 active seafarers in 2025, according to the shipping ministry.

title: Fuel Prices Hiked By Rs 7 In 11 Days. Why Petrol, Diesel Rates May Rise Again
content: For oil companies to fully recover past losses, petrol, diesel prices in India would theoretically need to rise by another Rs 28 to Rs 33 per litre.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei In Hiding, Using Courier Network To Communicate: Report
content: Khamenei, who was injured in US and Israeli strikes in Operation Epic Fury, has not been officially seen or heard in public since before the start of the war. Media reports claim he is taking extreme measures to avoid the strikes similar to the ones

title: In Twisha Sharma Case, Supreme Court's Big 'Narrative As Accused Ex-Judge' Remark
content: Twisha Sharma was found hanging at her matrimonial home in Bhopal, with her family accusing her husband, Samarth Singh, a lawyer, and mother-in-law Giribala Singh, a former district judge, of dowry harassment.

title: Fuel Price Hike: Why Petrol Costs Rs 117 In Hyderabad But Rs 102 In Delhi
content: Petrol, Diesel Price Today: States that need more revenue keep fuel taxes higher as fuel remains one of the most reliable source of tax collection.

title: Twisha Sharma Supreme Court Hearing LIVE Updates: Court Restrains Potential Witnesses, Accused From Giving Statements To Media
content: Twisha Sharma Death Case Reaches Supreme Court Hearing LIVE Updates: A Supreme Court bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Justice Vipul M Pancholi will hear the case.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 850 Points, Nifty Up 230 As Crude Oil Price Falls 5%
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: US crude fell over 8%, Brent down over 5% amid possibility of a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Telangana Woman, Lover Kill Husband For Being 'Obstacle', Bury Body In Farm
content: The accused woman, Kalpana, had filed a missing persons complaint against her husband, Muthyam Reddy (42), on May 18.

title: What makes Delhi Gymkhana so elite: Powerful legacy with Rs 200cr in mutual funds, Rs 129cr net worth
content: Delhi Gymkhana Club reported a 10-fold jump in surplus or profits to Rs 9.5 crore during 2023-24 from Rs 93 lakh in the previous year . it had a net worth of nearly Rs 129 crore and liquid funds to deal with possible threats of land lease not being renewed by the Centre .
